name: clone
context: Clone and fork GitHub repositories via git clone, gh repo clone, and the fork API
info: Clone GitHub repositories locally via HTTPS, SSH, or the GitHub CLI. Fork repositories to create personal copies for contribution. Supports shallow clones for large repos, sparse checkouts for monorepos, and configuring upstream remotes for fork-based workflows. Use `git clone`, `gh repo clone`, or `gh repo fork`. Forking creates a server-side copy linked to the upstream repo, enabling [[github/pulls]] back to the original. See [[github/auth]] for authentication and [[github/repos/create]] for creating new repos from scratch.
body:
# Clone and Fork Repositories

Get a local copy of a GitHub repository or create a fork for contribution.

## gh CLI

```bash
# Clone a repo (uses git protocol configured in gh)
gh repo clone owner/repo

# Clone into a specific directory
gh repo clone owner/repo my-local-dir

# Fork and clone in one step
gh repo fork owner/repo --clone

# Fork without cloning
gh repo fork owner/repo

# Fork to a specific org
gh repo fork owner/repo --org my-org
```

## git clone

```bash
# Clone via HTTPS
git clone https://github.com/owner/repo.git

# Clone via SSH (requires SSH key configured)
git clone git@github.com:owner/repo.git

# Clone specific branch
git clone -b develop https://github.com/owner/repo.git

# Shallow clone (last commit only — fast for large repos)
git clone --depth 1 https://github.com/owner/repo.git

# Shallow clone with limited history
git clone --depth 50 https://github.com/owner/repo.git

# Sparse checkout (only specific directories)
git clone --filter=blob:none --sparse https://github.com/owner/repo.git
cd repo
git sparse-checkout set src/api src/shared

# Mirror clone (exact copy including all refs)
git clone --mirror https://github.com/owner/repo.git
```

## Fork API

```javascript
import { Octokit } from "@octokit/rest";
const octokit = new Octokit({ auth: process.env.GITHUB_TOKEN });

// Fork a repository
const { data: fork } = await octokit.repos.createFork({
  owner: "upstream-owner",
  repo: "upstream-repo",
});
console.log(fork.html_url, fork.clone_url);

// Fork to an organization
const { data: orgFork } = await octokit.repos.createFork({
  owner: "upstream-owner",
  repo: "upstream-repo",
  organization: "my-org",
  name: "custom-fork-name",  // optional custom name
  default_branch_only: true, // only fork the default branch
});
```

## Fork Workflow

The standard open-source contribution workflow:

```bash
# 1. Fork and clone
gh repo fork upstream/repo --clone
cd repo

# 2. Verify remotes (origin = fork, upstream = original)
git remote -v
# origin    https://github.com/you/repo.git (fetch)
# upstream  https://github.com/upstream/repo.git (fetch)

# 3. Create feature branch
git checkout -b fix/typo-in-readme

# 4. Make changes, commit, push to your fork
git add .
git commit -m "fix: correct typo in README"
git push origin fix/typo-in-readme

# 5. Open PR from your fork to upstream
gh pr create --repo upstream/repo --title "Fix typo in README"

# 6. Keep fork in sync with upstream
git fetch upstream
git rebase upstream/main
git push origin main
```

## Clone Performance Tips

| Technique | Command | Use Case |
|-----------|---------|----------|
| Shallow clone | `--depth 1` | CI/CD, quick builds |
| Single branch | `--single-branch` | Only need one branch |
| Sparse checkout | `--filter=blob:none --sparse` | Monorepos, specific directories |
| Partial clone | `--filter=blob:none` | Large repos, lazy-load blobs |
| Mirror | `--mirror` | Backup, exact repository copy |

sections.title: Clone protocols
sections.body: HTTPS (`https://github.com/owner/repo.git`) works with PATs and credential managers. SSH (`git@github.com:owner/repo.git`) requires SSH keys configured in GitHub. HTTPS is simpler for CI/CD. SSH is preferred for interactive development. The `gh repo clone` command uses whatever protocol is configured via `gh config`.

sections.title: Forking repositories
sections.body: Fork creates a server-side copy of a repository under your account or organization. Forks maintain a link to the upstream repo, enabling PRs back to the original via [[github/pulls/create]]. Use `gh repo fork` or `octokit.repos.createFork()`. Set `default_branch_only: true` to only fork the default branch (saves space).

sections.title: Shallow and sparse clones
sections.body: Use `--depth N` for shallow clones that only fetch the last N commits. Use `--filter=blob:none --sparse` for monorepos where you only need specific directories. Shallow clones are faster and use less disk space. Partial clones (`--filter=blob:none`) lazy-load file contents on demand. These techniques are essential for CI/CD pipelines with large repositories.

links.context: Create repos from scratch instead of cloning
Open PRs from forks back to upstream

related.note: This is a sub-skill of the repos skill
